No Lands For You.flavor: Your opponent's lands aren't safe, their plans aren't safe, and by turn four neither is their will to live. Knights and Sengir Vampires clean up the rubble.

Mean Machine.flavor: An army of idiots with a king, a death wish, and Goblin Grenade. Blood Moon shows up just to ruin everyone else's afternoon.

Sliver Lining.flavor: Each new Sliver makes every other Sliver better, faster, and more obnoxious. Drop Coat of Arms and suddenly your opponent is doing math they didn't sign up for.

Dark N Stormy.flavor: No creatures, no subtlety, no plan B. Ball Lightning, Fork, Lightning Bolt — just a stack of red cards pointed at your opponent's face until the number hits zero.

Darwin's Doom.flavor: Fastbond and Gaea's Touch exist so Force of Nature can ruin someone's day on turn three. Fog is there for politeness.

Trick or Treat Freaks.flavor: Counter your spell, pocket the mana, sneak a free 12/12 onto the table via Illusionary Mask, and let Millstone handle whoever's still standing. Mystical Tutor finds exactly what's needed, exactly when it's needed.

Folk Music.flavor: Lord of Atlantis gives the whole squad Islandwalk, Phantasmal Terrain turns your opponent's lands into Islands, and suddenly your fish are unblockable. Energy Flux is just there to ruin someone's artifact-based day.

Holy War.flavor: Crusade, Jihad, White Knights — a self-righteous beatdown that hits harder than it has any right to. Meekstone and Kismet make sure anything bigger than your creatures stays politely tapped down forever.

Clockwork.flavor: Assemble the Urza's lands, produce seven colorless mana, and deploy an army of mechanical nightmares that don't care what color you're playing. Rocket Launcher handles the leftovers.

Bone Dry.flavor: Hymn to Tourach, Mind Twist, Hypnotic Specter — the hand's gone before they knew what hit them. The Rack and Megrim spend the rest of the game making them regret every card they draw.

The Emerald Army.flavor: Llanowar Elves make mana, Fyndhorn Elves make mana, Kaysa makes everyone scarier, and Overrun makes the math embarrassing. Ice Storm and Thermokarst are for opponents who had the audacity to keep lands.

Swamp Things.flavor: Buried Alive finds Crimson Hellkite and Spirit of the Night; Living Death puts them all into play at once and wipes whatever was already there. A completely unreasonable amount of dragons for this early in the game.

Demolition Derby.flavor: Survival of the Fittest fills the graveyard, Recurring Nightmare loops the best creatures back on a permanent subscription. Grave Pact plus Sengir Autocrat's token factory means your opponent is sacrificing their board whether they like it or not.

Hand Grenade.flavor: Force everyone to draw constantly with Howling Mine and Teferi's Puzzle Box, let Underworld Dreams charge them for the privilege, then Winter Orb and Relic Barrier make sure they're too mana-starved to do anything about it. Chaos, but on purpose.

Mist Monsters.flavor: Serendib Efreet hits fast while Counterspells handle any objections. If that's not enough, there's always a free 12/12 Dreadnought hiding behind an Illusionary Mask for special occasions.

Bad Ass Fault.flavor: Hypnotic Specter eats the hand while Stone Rain eats the lands. By the time your opponent stabilizes, there's nothing left to stabilize with.

Extinction.flavor: Kird Ape and Lhurgoyf arrive early and hit hard; Dense Foliage makes them immune to targeted removal. If things get complicated, Wrath of God or Cataclysm reminds everyone who's actually in charge.

Smashed Potatoes.flavor: Swarm the board, build a hand with Land Tax, then Armageddon the whole table when you're ahead. Your opponent rebuilds from nothing. You don't.

YCBI.flavor: Soraya the Falconer leads a sky full of Falcons and Angels that nothing can block. Counterspell and Kismet handle the ground game so your air force can operate without interference.

Frozen Stiffs.flavor: A slow, artifact-powered stranglehold that keeps creatures tapped, mana locked, and options nonexistent. Wins by making the game miserable to play into.

Glop Artists.flavor: Cataclysm strips the board to almost nothing, Balance finishes the cleanup, and Argivian Archaeologist recycles the pieces that keep the prison running. A deck that wins by having slightly more than zero.

Icy Hot.flavor: Icy Manipulator taps anything threatening, Propaganda taxes attacks into irrelevance, and the Red splash exists mostly to keep things interesting. Control that charges admission for the privilege of playing against it.

Murphy's Law.flavor: Angelic Renewal keeps looping creatures back from the grave while enchantments stack up and make everything stickier. Harder to kill than it has any right to be.

The Attritionists.flavor: Bad Moon and Breeding Pit generate a constant stream of black bodies, and Grave Pact means every sacrifice takes something with it. The deck doesn't win so much as outlast everything else.

Big Gloves.flavor: Red. Black. Chain Lightning to the face. No further questions.

Fire Drilled.flavor: Dragon Whelp takes to the skies early and Earthquake clears whatever's blocking the runway. A Red deck with zero patience for subtlety.

Peak-a-Boom.flavor: Birds of Paradise accelerates the curve while Blue counters protect the investment. The Green/Blue deck that wants to be ahead on board and never let you catch up.

Overdrawn.flavor: Arcane Denial handles threats while Teferi's Puzzle Box scrambles everyone's hand each turn. Wins by being slightly more comfortable with total chaos than whoever it's playing against.

Thrull Frottle.flavor: Basal Thrull sacrifices for mana, Armor Thrull hands out +1/+1 counters, and the whole messy engine grinds forward on vibes and recursion. Not glamorous. Effective.

The Doldrums.flavor: Counter first, second and third, then mill away once their initial strike has been sapped. The League's first mono-blue experiment in the making — resource denial through patience, counters, and Dreadnoughts waiting in the wings.

Team 10.flavor: Get Yawgmoth Demon into play. Everything else exists to support that singular, terrifying ambition. Black Vise punishes full hands, Winter Orb slows the world down, and when the Demon arrives, the game changes permanently.

Black & Blueses.flavor: Hand, library, creatures, spells — everything goes. Slow the game to a crawl, eliminate the opponent's library card by card, and punish them for discarding anything. B&B wants a drain of a game, and will counter and kill anything that seeks to stop them.

Dead Heads.flavor: Goblins and skeletons make for an awful nice Halloween scare. A battlefield full of them guarantees a spooky cocktail of unrestrained mayhem.

El Kamino.flavor: Summon creatures, mask their identities, clone them, then hit the attack button. An army of clones is fine. An army of clones of the single largest creature in the game? Maybe bring your rabbit's foot.

Mystery Flavor.flavor: One thing is for certain — there is absolutely no core objective for this group of old favorites. Similar to Team 10, the Flavors are a mish-mosh of great cards sitting idly by on the sidelines. Kind of like how a fruit cake is made, which begs the question: does anyone even like fruit cake?

Team 11.flavor: Full disclosure — we'd like to get Yawgmoth Demon in play. Is he here? Yawggy? The entire roster is built around him and belle-to-be Titania's Song. A real grab bag of cards that could've been good, but were not.

Green Boss.flavor: The biggest, most ridiculous group of green creatures imaginable. Forget lions, tigers and bears — you've got way bigger problems. This team is unfair, and we know it. An apology is probably owed to everyone who's faced it.

Red Boss.flavor: Dragons, goblins, and everything that burns. Modern red at its most unhinged — faster, bigger, and more relentless than anything the original card pool could produce. May require some tinkering.

Black Boss.flavor: Undead zombies, vampires, nightmarish beasts and demons. Modern black creatures that make the League's discard strategies look quaint. Bone-men dealing the lion's share of damage, moving through opponents like a warm knife through butter.
